description: A 'rinoka' is a bounded undertaking that gathers related tasks towards a stated result. Its boundary may change as people learn what the work requires. 'winora' is the plan and 'seru' a person's commitment; neither is built into the project.
articulatory_notes: Open with short alveolar 'r', made as one tap or a brief trill, and high-front 'i'. Voiced dental 'n' rests at the upper teeth while nasal airflow carries into stressed true-mid rounded 'o'. The final syllable closes at the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and releases into central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: Opening 'ri' places the word beside 'riola' (labor) and 'riporu' (task) in the ear. Rounded 'no' holds the middle before final 'ka' releases the form into a broad close.
usage_notes: A project is countable. Purpose and boundary identify it, while participants, authority, acceptance, and resources remain separate relations. One project may contain several tasks and more than one plan.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A community project can gather repair or ecological work under one purpose while its plan and boundaries remain open to revision.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Keeping the undertaking separate from commitment and authority prevents a shared project from becoming blanket consent.

semantic_domains.activity: a bounded undertaking made from related tasks
semantic_domains.community: a frame within which people may coordinate their participation
semantic_domains.creation: work gathered towards a stated result
